message:
perf(pager): replace sharded RwLock published-page map with direct-index atomic slots

The published-page store previously used 64 RwLock-sharded HashMaps to
allow concurrent reads across page number ranges. While this eliminated the
single-lock bottleneck, reader threads still contended on per-shard RwLock
acquisitions during hot-path page lookups.

This commit introduces AtomicPublishedPages: a direct-index slot array for
page numbers 1–65535 (the hot set covering virtually all B-tree interior
and early leaf pages). Each slot uses an AtomicBool present flag with
Acquire/Release ordering so readers can fast-path reject absent pages
without ever touching a Mutex. When the page IS present, a per-slot Mutex
protects the Option<PageData> clone — but this path is only taken on cache
hits, not misses.

Key design points:
- 65535 slots × (AtomicBool + Mutex<Option<PageData>>) — the PageData is
  an Arc<[u8]> so the Option is pointer-sized when None
- insert/remove are called under the existing publish_lock so the atomic
  state word only coordinates readers with a single active writer
- page_count tracked with AtomicUsize for O(1) len queries
- Pages above the limit fall through to the existing sharded map (retained
  but now cold-path only)
- clear() sweeps all slots on transaction rollback/checkpoint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
